Question:
We will plan to get a weighted prevalence using exam weights from NHANES data from 2001-2008.
Include participants who meet any of the diabetes criteria: self-reported diabetes diagnosis is yes, glycohemoglobin (HbA1C) >= 6.5%, or fasting glucoes (mg/dl)>=126. 
We will exclude participates whose age at screening are equal or less than 20 years old. We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl). 
We will get a prevalence of NA-Normal which is defined as havinig both an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) equal or more than 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 and urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) of less than 30 mg/g.
eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 
1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159
2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018
3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159
4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)
urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).

-- STEP 1: Create temp_master_weight

-- TEMP TABLE FOR weight
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_weight;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_weight AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_4_year_mec_exam_weight * 4.0 / 10.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_b
WHERE full_sample_4_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 10.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_c
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 10.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_d
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number,

    t2."fasting_glucose_mg_dl",
    t3."age_in_years_at_screening",
    t4."gender",
    t5."race_hispanic_origin",
    t6."albumin_urine_mg_l",
    t7."creatinine_urine_mg_dl",
    t8."creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM temp_all_ids a
LEFT JOIN temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes t0 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t0.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_glycohemoglobin t1 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t1.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_fasting_glucose_mg_dl t2 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t2.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening t3 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t3.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_gender t4 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t4.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_race_hispanic_origin t5 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t5.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l t6 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t6.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl t7 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t7.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl t8 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t8.respondent_sequence_number;

CREATE INDEX idx_final_master_respondent_sequence_number    
    ON final_master_table (respondent_sequence_number);


-- Clean up final_master_table
DELETE FROM final_master_table
WHERE respondent_sequence_number NOT IN (
    SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM weight_design
);


-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients who meet any of the diabetes criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
   OR "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5
   OR "fasting_glucose_mg_dl" >= 126;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening are equal or less than 20 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" > 20 OR f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who are missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants who are missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants who are missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants who are missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants who are missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Outcome: na_normal
-- Calculate egfr (ml/min/1.73m^2)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Determine na_normal
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_na_normal AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN t_egfr."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" >= 60 AND t_uacr."urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_mg_g" < 30 THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "na_normal"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_egfr t_egfr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_egfr."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_uacr t_uacr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_uacr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  t_na."na_normal"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_na_normal t_na ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = t_na."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

-- STEP: Create merged final table with design variables + in_analysis flag
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_final_with_weights;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_with_weights AS
SELECT 
    wd."respondent_sequence_number",
    wd."masked_variance_pseudo_psu",
    wd."masked_variance_pseudo_stratum",
    wd."survey_weight",
    CASE WHEN ft."respondent_sequence_number" IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS "in_analysis",
    ft."na_normal"
FROM weight_design wd
LEFT JOIN temp_final_table ft
  ON wd."respondent_sequence_number" = ft."respondent_sequence_number";

R:
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(broom))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tibble))

    identifier_vars <- c("respondent_sequence_number")
    outcome_vars <- c("na_normal")
    weight_vars <- c("survey_weight","masked_variance_pseudo_psu","masked_variance_pseudo_stratum","in_analysis")

    cohort <- df[df$in_analysis == 1, names(df) != "in_analysis"]


    outcome_roles <- c()
    outcome_roles["na_normal"] <- "outcome"


    summarize_variable <- function(column, name) {
    n_unique <- length(unique(na.omit(column)))
    na_count <- sum(is.na(column))

    if (name %in% identifier_vars) {
        category <- "identifier"
        summary_info <- paste0("Total rows = ", nrow(cohort))

    } else if (name %in% outcome_vars) {
        # Assign its special role (e.g., "outcome", "mediator")
        category <- ifelse(!is.na(outcome_roles[name]), outcome_roles[name], "outcome")

        # Generate a summary based on its underlying data type
        underlying_type <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (underlying_type == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (underlying_type %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }

    

    } else if (name %in% weight_vars) {
        category <- "weight"
        summary_info <- "Weight variable" 
    

    } else {
        # This block handles regular predictors
        category <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (category == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (category %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }
    }

    data.frame(
        variable   = name,
        category   = category,
        n_unique   = n_unique,
        na_count   = na_count,
        summary    = summary_info,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    }

    report <- bind_rows(
    lapply(names(cohort), function(col_name) {
        summarize_variable(cohort[[col_name]], col_name)
    })
    )

    identifier_vars  <- report$variable[report$category == "identifier"]
    cat("Identifier Variables:\n");   print(identifier_vars);   cat("\n")

    weight_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "weight"]; cat("Weight Variables: "); print(weight_vars); psu_var <- weight_vars[grepl("psu", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("PSU:", psu_var); stratum_var <- weight_vars[grepl("stratum", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("Stratum:", stratum_var); weight_var <- weight_vars[grepl("weight", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("Weight:", weight_var); in_analysis_var <- weight_vars[grepl("in_analysis", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("In Analysis:", in_analysis_var)

    binary_vars      <- report$variable[report$category == "binary"]
    cat("Binary Variables:\n");       print(binary_vars);       cat("\n")
    print(binary_vars)

    categorical_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "categorical"]
    cat("Categorical Variables:\n");  print(categorical_vars);  cat("\n")
    print(categorical_vars)

    numerical_vars   <- report$variable[report$category == "numerical"]
    cat("Numerical Variables:\n");    print(numerical_vars);    cat("\n")
    print(numerical_vars)

    
    

    outcome_var     <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("outcome")]
    cat("Outcome Variables:\n");      print(outcome_var);      cat("\n")
    

    
    library(survey)
    options(survey.lonely.psu = "adjust")
        
    
    group_vars_list <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary")]
    
    # Loop through the variables and ensure they are clean factors in the 'df' dataframe.
    for (var in group_vars_list) {
    if (var %in% names(df)) {
        df[[var]] <- factor(trimws(as.character(df[[var]])))
    }
    }
        
    
    df[[outcome_var]] <- ifelse(tolower(df[[outcome_var]]) == "yes", 1,
                    ifelse(tolower(df[[outcome_var]]) == "no", 0,
                    ifelse(grepl("1", as.character(df[[outcome_var]])), 1,
                    ifelse(grepl("0", as.character(df[[outcome_var]])), 0,
                            as.numeric(df[[outcome_var]])))))


    varformula <- as.formula(paste0("~", outcome_var))
        
    
    survey_design <- svydesign(
        ids     = as.formula(paste0("~", psu_var)),
        strata  = as.formula(paste0("~", stratum_var)),
        weights = as.formula(paste0("~", weight_var)),
        data    = df,
        nest    = TRUE
        )
    survey_design <- subset(survey_design, in_analysis ==1)
        
    
    # Calculate Overall Prevalence.
    total_prev <- svymean(varformula, survey_design, na.rm = TRUE)
    total_conf <- confint(total_prev)

    # Initialize a list to store pairwise results
    pairwise_results <- list()

    # Loop through each grouping variable
    all_strat_results <- lapply(group_vars_list, function(current_group_var) {

        byformula <- as.formula(paste0("~", current_group_var))
        test_formula <- as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ ", current_group_var))

        # Prevalence and confidence intervals
        strat_prev <- svyby(varformula, byformula, survey_design, svymean, na.rm = TRUE)
        strat_conf <- confint(strat_prev)

        strat_result <- as_tibble(strat_prev) %>%
            cbind(strat_conf) %>%
            rename(
                Group = 1,
                prevalence = 2,
                conf_low = `2.5 %`,
                conf_high = `97.5 %`
            ) %>%
            mutate(Variable = current_group_var, .before = 1)

        # --- Global P-value ---
        group_levels <- levels(factor(df[[current_group_var]]))
        num_levels <- length(group_levels)
        global_p_value <- NA_real_

        test_result <- NULL
        test_p <- NA_real_

        if (num_levels == 2) {
            test_result <- tryCatch(
                svyttest(test_formula, survey_design),
                error = function(e) NULL
            )
        } else if (num_levels > 2) {
            chisq_formula <- as.formula(paste0("~", outcome_var, " + ", current_group_var))
            test_result <- tryCatch(
                svychisq(chisq_formula, survey_design),
                error = function(e) NULL
            )
        }

        if (!is.null(test_result)) {
            test_p <- tryCatch(test_result$p.value, error = function(e) NA_real_)
        }
        global_p_value <- test_p

        # Add global p-value only to the first row
        strat_result$`P-value` <- ""
        strat_result$`P-value`[1] <- if (!is.na(global_p_value)) round(global_p_value, 4) else NA_character_

        # Keep only the columns needed in the final result table
        strat_result <- strat_result %>%
            dplyr::select(Variable, Group, prevalence, conf_low, conf_high, `P-value`)

        # --- Pairwise p-values table (kept as before) ---
        pairwise_df <- tibble(
            Variable = character(),
            Comparison = character(),
            `P-value` = numeric()
        )

        if (num_levels > 2) {
            all_pairs <- combn(group_levels, 2, simplify = FALSE)
            for (pair in all_pairs) {
                pair_name <- paste(pair[1], "vs", pair[2], sep = "_")
                pair_design <- subset(survey_design, get(current_group_var) %in% pair)
                test_result <- try(svyttest(test_formula, pair_design), silent = TRUE)
                if (!inherits(test_result, "try-error")) {
                    pairwise_df <- bind_rows(pairwise_df, tibble(
                        Variable = current_group_var,
                        Comparison = pair_name,
                        `P-value` = round(test_result$p.value, 4)
                    ))
                }
            }
        }

        # Store in global list
        pairwise_results[[current_group_var]] <<- pairwise_df

        return(strat_result)
    })

    
    # Combine into a final result table
    total_result_df <- tibble(
        Variable = "Overall",
        Group = "Overall",
        prevalence = as.numeric(coef(total_prev)),
        conf_low = as.numeric(total_conf[1]),
        conf_high = as.numeric(total_conf[2]),
        `P-value` = ""
    )

    combined_strat_df <- bind_rows(all_strat_results)
    result_table <- bind_rows(total_result_df, combined_strat_df) %>%
        dplyr::select(Variable, Group, prevalence, conf_low, conf_high, `P-value`)

    # Combine pairwise p-values into one data frame (unchanged)
    pairwise_pvalue_table <- bind_rows(pairwise_results)

We will plan to get a weighted prevalence using exam weights from NHANES data from 2001-2008.
Include participants who meet any of the diabetes criteria: self-reported diabetes diagnosis is yes, glycohemoglobin (HbA1C) >= 6.5%, or fasting glucoes (mg/dl)>=126. 
We will exclude participates whose age at screening are equal or less than 20 years old. We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl). 
We will get a prevalence of NA-Normal which is defined as havinig both an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) equal or more than 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 and urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) of less than 30 mg/g.
eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 
1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159

3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159
4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)
urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).

SQL:
-- STEP 1: Create temp_master_weight

-- TEMP TABLE FOR weight
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_weight;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_weight AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_4_year_mec_exam_weight * 4.0 / 10.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_b
WHERE full_sample_4_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 10.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_c
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 10.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_d
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 10.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_e
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL;


-- STEP 2: Create temp_psu_stratum

-- TEMP TABLE FOR PSU and Stratum
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_psu_stratum;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_psu_stratum AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_c
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_e;


-- STEP 3: Create final weight+design table
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS weight_design;
CREATE TABLE weight_design AS
SELECT 
    s.respondent_sequence_number,
    s.masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
    s.masked_variance_pseudo_stratum,
    w.survey_weight
FROM temp_psu_stratum s
INNER JOIN temp_master_weight w
  ON s.respondent_sequence_number = w.respondent_sequence_number;




-- TEMP TABLE FOR "self-reported diabetes diagnosis"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes ON temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "glycohemoglobin (hba1c) (%)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_glycohemoglobin;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_glycohemoglobin AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.ghb_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.ghb_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.l10_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.l10_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_glycohemoglobin ON temp_master_glycohemoglobin (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "fasting glucoes (mg/dl)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_fasting_glucose_mg_dl;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_fasting_glucose_mg_dl AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "fasting_glucose_mg_dl" AS "fasting_glucose_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.glu_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "fasting_glucose_mg_dl" AS "fasting_glucose_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.glu_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "fasting_glucose_mg_dl" AS "fasting_glucose_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.l10am_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "fasting_glucose_mg_dl" AS "fasting_glucose_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.l10am_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_fasting_glucose_mg_dl ON temp_master_fasting_glucose_mg_dl (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "age at screening", "age at screening"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening ON temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "gender", "gender"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_gender;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_gender AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "gender" AS "gender"
FROM nhanes.demo_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "gender" AS "gender"
FROM nhanes.demo_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "gender" AS "gender"
FROM nhanes.demo_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "gender" AS "gender"
FROM nhanes.demo_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_gender ON temp_master_gender (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "race/hispanic origin", "race/hispanic origin"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_race_hispanic_origin;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "race_hispanic_origin" AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM nhanes.demo_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "race_hispanic_origin" AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM nhanes.demo_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "race_hispanic_origin" AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM nhanes.demo_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "race_hispanic_origin" AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM nhanes.demo_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_race_hispanic_origin ON temp_master_race_hispanic_origin (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "urine albumin (mg/l)", "urine albumin (mg/l)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_urine_mg_l" AS "albumin_urine_mg_l"
FROM nhanes.alb_cr_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_urine_mg_l" AS "albumin_urine_mg_l"
FROM nhanes.alb_cr_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_urine_mg_l_si" AS "albumin_urine_mg_l"
FROM nhanes.l16_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_urine_mg_l_si" AS "albumin_urine_mg_l"
FROM nhanes.l16_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l ON temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "urine creatinine (mg/dl)", "urine creatinine (mg/dl)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_urine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_urine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.alb_cr_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_urine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_urine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.alb_cr_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_urine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_urine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.l16_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_urine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_urine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.l16_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl ON temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "serum creatinine (mg/dl)", "serum creatinine (mg/dl)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.l40_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.l40_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl ON temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl (respondent_sequence_number);

-- ALL UNIQUE PATIENT IDs
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_all_ids;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_all_ids AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_glycohemoglobin
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_fasting_glucose_mg_dl
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_gender
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_race_hispanic_origin
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl;

-- Add index to the main ID table for the final join
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_all_ids_respondent_sequence_number ON temp_all_ids (respondent_sequence_number);

-- FINAL MASTER TABLE
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS final_master_table;
CREATE TABLE final_master_table AS
SELECT
    a.respondent_sequence_number,
    t0."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes",
    t1."glycohemoglobin",
    t2."fasting_glucose_mg_dl",
    t3."age_in_years_at_screening",
    t4."gender",
    t5."race_hispanic_origin",
    t6."albumin_urine_mg_l",
    t7."creatinine_urine_mg_dl",
    t8."creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM temp_all_ids a
LEFT JOIN temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes t0 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t0.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_glycohemoglobin t1 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t1.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_fasting_glucose_mg_dl t2 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t2.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening t3 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t3.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_gender t4 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t4.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_race_hispanic_origin t5 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t5.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l t6 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t6.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl t7 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t7.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl t8 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t8.respondent_sequence_number;

CREATE INDEX idx_final_master_respondent_sequence_number    
    ON final_master_table (respondent_sequence_number);


-- Clean up final_master_table
DELETE FROM final_master_table
WHERE respondent_sequence_number NOT IN (
    SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM weight_design
);


-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes patients with self-reported diabetes diagnosis = 'Yes'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1_diabetes_diagnosis AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes';

-- STEP 1.2: Includes patients with glycohemoglobin (HbA1C) >= 6.5%
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2_hba1c AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5;

-- STEP 1.3: Includes patients with fasting glucose (mg/dl) >= 126
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step3_fasting_glucose AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "fasting_glucose_mg_dl" >= 126;

-- STEP 1.4: Combine all inclusion criteria using OR logic
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_step1_diabetes_diagnosis
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_step2_hba1c
UNION
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number" FROM temp_inclusion_step3_fasting_glucose;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients whose age at screening are equal or less than 20 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" > 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who are missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants who are missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants who are missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants who are missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants who are missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Outcome: na_normal
-- STEP 4.1.1: Calculate eGFR (mL/min/1.73m^2)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141.0 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1.0), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1.0), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141.0 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1.0), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1.0), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141.0 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1.0), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1.0), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141.0 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1.0), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1.0), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.1.2: Calculate urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr_raw AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  (100.0 * (f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl")) AS "urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.1.3: Determine na_normal based on eGFR and UACR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_na_normal_boolean AS
SELECT
  e."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" >= 60 AND u."urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_mg_g" < 30 THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS "na_normal_boolean"
FROM temp_egfr_raw e
JOIN temp_uacr_raw u ON e."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.1.4: Map na_normal to 1 or 0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_na_normal AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "na_normal_boolean" = TRUE THEN 1
    WHEN "na_normal_boolean" = FALSE THEN 0
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "na_normal"
FROM temp_na_normal_boolean;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  n."na_normal"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_na_normal n ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = n."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

-- STEP: Create merged final table with design variables + in_analysis flag
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_final_with_weights;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_with_weights AS
SELECT 
    wd."respondent_sequence_number",
    wd."masked_variance_pseudo_psu",
    wd."masked_variance_pseudo_stratum",
    wd."survey_weight",
    CASE WHEN ft."respondent_sequence_number" IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS "in_analysis",
    ft."na_normal"
FROM weight_design wd
LEFT JOIN temp_final_table ft
  ON wd."respondent_sequence_number" = ft."respondent_sequence_number";
  
-- Preview the result

R:
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(broom))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tibble))

    identifier_vars <- c("respondent_sequence_number")
    outcome_vars <- c("na_normal")
    weight_vars <- c("survey_weight","masked_variance_pseudo_psu","masked_variance_pseudo_stratum","in_analysis")

    cohort <- df[df$in_analysis == 1, names(df) != "in_analysis"]


    outcome_roles <- c()
    outcome_roles["na_normal"] <- "outcome"


    summarize_variable <- function(column, name) {
    n_unique <- length(unique(na.omit(column)))
    na_count <- sum(is.na(column))

    if (name %in% identifier_vars) {
        category <- "identifier"
        summary_info <- paste0("Total rows = ", nrow(cohort))

    } else if (name %in% outcome_vars) {
        # Assign its special role (e.g., "outcome", "mediator")
        category <- ifelse(!is.na(outcome_roles[name]), outcome_roles[name], "outcome")

        # Generate a summary based on its underlying data type
        underlying_type <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (underlying_type == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (underlying_type %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }

    

    } else if (name %in% weight_vars) {
        category <- "weight"
        summary_info <- "Weight variable" 
    

    } else {
        # This block handles regular predictors
        category <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (category == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (category %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }
    }

    data.frame(
        variable   = name,
        category   = category,
        n_unique   = n_unique,
        na_count   = na_count,
        summary    = summary_info,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    }

    report <- bind_rows(
    lapply(names(cohort), function(col_name) {
        summarize_variable(cohort[[col_name]], col_name)
    })
    )

    identifier_vars  <- report$variable[report$category == "identifier"]
    cat("Identifier Variables:\n");   print(identifier_vars);   cat("\n")

    weight_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "weight"]; cat("Weight Variables: "); print(weight_vars); psu_var <- weight_vars[grepl("psu", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("PSU:", psu_var); stratum_var <- weight_vars[grepl("stratum", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("Stratum:", stratum_var); weight_var <- weight_vars[grepl("weight", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("Weight:", weight_var); in_analysis_var <- weight_vars[grepl("in_analysis", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("In Analysis:", in_analysis_var)

    binary_vars      <- report$variable[report$category == "binary"]
    cat("Binary Variables:\n");       print(binary_vars);       cat("\n")
    print(binary_vars)

    categorical_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "categorical"]
    cat("Categorical Variables:\n");  print(categorical_vars);  cat("\n")
    print(categorical_vars)

    numerical_vars   <- report$variable[report$category == "numerical"]
    cat("Numerical Variables:\n");    print(numerical_vars);    cat("\n")
    print(numerical_vars)

    
    

    outcome_var     <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("outcome")]
    cat("Outcome Variables:\n");      print(outcome_var);      cat("\n")
    

    
    library(survey)
    options(survey.lonely.psu = "adjust")
        
    
    group_vars_list <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary")]
    
    # Loop through the variables and ensure they are clean factors in the 'df' dataframe.
    for (var in group_vars_list) {
    if (var %in% names(df)) {
        df[[var]] <- factor(trimws(as.character(df[[var]])))
    }
    }
        
    
    df[[outcome_var]] <- ifelse(tolower(df[[outcome_var]]) == "yes", 1,
                    ifelse(tolower(df[[outcome_var]]) == "no", 0,
                    ifelse(grepl("1", as.character(df[[outcome_var]])), 1,
                    ifelse(grepl("0", as.character(df[[outcome_var]])), 0,
                            as.numeric(df[[outcome_var]])))))


    varformula <- as.formula(paste0("~", outcome_var))
        
    
    survey_design <- svydesign(
        ids     = as.formula(paste0("~", psu_var)),
        strata  = as.formula(paste0("~", stratum_var)),
        weights = as.formula(paste0("~", weight_var)),
        data    = df,
        nest    = TRUE
        )
    survey_design <- subset(survey_design, in_analysis ==1)
        
    
    # Calculate Overall Prevalence.
    total_prev <- svymean(varformula, survey_design, na.rm = TRUE)
    total_conf <- confint(total_prev)

    # Initialize a list to store pairwise results
    pairwise_results <- list()

    # Loop through each grouping variable
    all_strat_results <- lapply(group_vars_list, function(current_group_var) {

        byformula <- as.formula(paste0("~", current_group_var))
        test_formula <- as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ ", current_group_var))

        # Prevalence and confidence intervals
        strat_prev <- svyby(varformula, byformula, survey_design, svymean, na.rm = TRUE)
        strat_conf <- confint(strat_prev)

        strat_result <- as_tibble(strat_prev) %>%
            cbind(strat_conf) %>%
            rename(
                Group = 1,
                prevalence = 2,
                conf_low = `2.5 %`,
                conf_high = `97.5 %`
            ) %>%
            mutate(Variable = current_group_var, .before = 1)

        # --- Global P-value ---
        group_levels <- levels(factor(df[[current_group_var]]))
        num_levels <- length(group_levels)
        global_p_value <- NA_real_

        test_result <- NULL
        test_p <- NA_real_

        if (num_levels == 2) {
            test_result <- tryCatch(
                svyttest(test_formula, survey_design),
                error = function(e) NULL
            )
        } else if (num_levels > 2) {
            chisq_formula <- as.formula(paste0("~", outcome_var, " + ", current_group_var))
            test_result <- tryCatch(
                svychisq(chisq_formula, survey_design),
                error = function(e) NULL
            )
        }

        if (!is.null(test_result)) {
            test_p <- tryCatch(test_result$p.value, error = function(e) NA_real_)
        }
        global_p_value <- test_p

        # Add global p-value only to the first row
        strat_result$`P-value` <- ""
        strat_result$`P-value`[1] <- if (!is.na(global_p_value)) round(global_p_value, 4) else NA_character_

        # Keep only the columns needed in the final result table
        strat_result <- strat_result %>%
            dplyr::select(Variable, Group, prevalence, conf_low, conf_high, `P-value`)

        # --- Pairwise p-values table (kept as before) ---
        pairwise_df <- tibble(
            Variable = character(),
            Comparison = character(),
            `P-value` = numeric()
        )

        if (num_levels > 2) {
            all_pairs <- combn(group_levels, 2, simplify = FALSE)
            for (pair in all_pairs) {
                pair_name <- paste(pair[1], "vs", pair[2], sep = "_")
                pair_design <- subset(survey_design, get(current_group_var) %in% pair)
                test_result <- try(svyttest(test_formula, pair_design), silent = TRUE)
                if (!inherits(test_result, "try-error")) {
                    pairwise_df <- bind_rows(pairwise_df, tibble(
                        Variable = current_group_var,
                        Comparison = pair_name,
                        `P-value` = round(test_result$p.value, 4)
                    ))
                }
            }
        }

        # Store in global list
        pairwise_results[[current_group_var]] <<- pairwise_df

        return(strat_result)
    })

    
    # Combine into a final result table
    total_result_df <- tibble(
        Variable = "Overall",
        Group = "Overall",
        prevalence = as.numeric(coef(total_prev)),
        conf_low = as.numeric(total_conf[1]),
        conf_high = as.numeric(total_conf[2]),
        `P-value` = ""
    )

    combined_strat_df <- bind_rows(all_strat_results)
    result_table <- bind_rows(total_result_df, combined_strat_df) %>%
        dplyr::select(Variable, Group, prevalence, conf_low, conf_high, `P-value`)

    # Combine pairwise p-values into one data frame (unchanged)
    pairwise_pvalue_table <- bind_rows(pairwise_results)

Question:
We will plan to get a weighted prevalence using exam weights from NHANES data from 2001-2008.
Include participants who meet any of the diabetes criteria: self-reported diabetes diagnosis is yes, glycohemoglobin (HbA1C) >= 6.5%, or fasting glucoes (mg/dl)>=126. 
We will exclude participates whose age at screening are equal or less than 20 years old. We will exclude participants who are missing gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl). 
We will get a prevalence of NA-Normal which is defined as havinig both an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) equal or more than 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 and urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) of less than 30 mg/g.
eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 
1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159
2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018
3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159
4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)
urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2001-2008

- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing urine albumin (mg/L)

       141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)

SQL:
-- STEP 1: Create temp_master_weight

-- TEMP TABLE FOR weight
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_weight;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_weight AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_4_year_mec_exam_weight * 4.0 / 10.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_b
WHERE full_sample_4_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 10.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_c
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 10.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_d
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 10.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_e
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL;


-- STEP 2: Create temp_psu_stratum

-- TEMP TABLE FOR PSU and Stratum
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_psu_stratum;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_psu_stratum AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_c
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_e;


-- STEP 3: Create final weight+design table
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS weight_design;
CREATE TABLE weight_design AS
SELECT 
    s.respondent_sequence_number,
    s.masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
    s.masked_variance_pseudo_stratum,
    w.survey_weight
FROM temp_psu_stratum s
INNER JOIN temp_master_weight w
  ON s.respondent_sequence_number = w.respondent_sequence_number;




-- TEMP TABLE FOR "self-reported diabetes diagnosis"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes ON temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "glycohemoglobin (hba1c) (%)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_glycohemoglobin;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_glycohemoglobin AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.ghb_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.ghb_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.l10_b
UNION ALL

-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_glycohemoglobin ON temp_master_glycohemoglobin (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "fasting glucoes (mg/dl)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_fasting_glucose_mg_dl;

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "age at screening (years old)", "age at screening (years old)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening;

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "gender", "gender"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_gender;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_gender AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "gender" AS "gender"
FROM nhanes.demo_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "gender" AS "gender"
FROM nhanes.demo_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "gender" AS "gender"
FROM nhanes.demo_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "gender" AS "gender"
FROM nhanes.demo_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_gender ON temp_master_gender (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "race/hispanic origin", "race/hispanic origin"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_race_hispanic_origin;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "race_hispanic_origin" AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM nhanes.demo_d
UNION ALL

FROM nhanes.demo_e

FROM nhanes.demo_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "race_hispanic_origin" AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM nhanes.demo_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_race_hispanic_origin ON temp_master_race_hispanic_origin (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "urine albumin (mg/l)", "urine albumin (mg/l)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_urine_mg_l" AS "albumin_urine_mg_l"
FROM nhanes.alb_cr_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_urine_mg_l" AS "albumin_urine_mg_l"
FROM nhanes.alb_cr_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_urine_mg_l_si" AS "albumin_urine_mg_l"
FROM nhanes.l16_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_urine_mg_l_si" AS "albumin_urine_mg_l"
FROM nhanes.l16_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l ON temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "urine creatinine (mg/dl)", "urine creatinine (mg/dl)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_urine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_urine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.alb_cr_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_urine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_urine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.alb_cr_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_urine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_urine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.l16_b
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_urine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_urine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.l16_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl ON temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "serum creatinine (mg/dl)", "serum creatinine (mg/dl)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_d
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_e
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.l40_b
UNION ALL

FROM nhanes.l40_c;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl ON temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl (respondent_sequence_number);

-- ALL UNIQUE PATIENT IDs
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_all_ids;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_all_ids AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_glycohemoglobin
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_fasting_glucose_mg_dl
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_gender
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_race_hispanic_origin
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl;

-- Add index to the main ID table for the final join
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_all_ids_respondent_sequence_number ON temp_all_ids (respondent_sequence_number);

-- FINAL MASTER TABLE
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS final_master_table;
CREATE TABLE final_master_table AS
SELECT
    a.respondent_sequence_number,
    t0."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes",
    t1."glycohemoglobin",
    t2."fasting_glucose_mg_dl",
    t3."age_in_years_at_screening",
    t4."gender",
    t5."race_hispanic_origin",
    t6."albumin_urine_mg_l",
    t7."creatinine_urine_mg_dl",
    t8."creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM temp_all_ids a
LEFT JOIN temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes t0 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t0.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_glycohemoglobin t1 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t1.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_fasting_glucose_mg_dl t2 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t2.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening t3 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t3.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_gender t4 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t4.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_race_hispanic_origin t5 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t5.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l t6 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t6.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl t7 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t7.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl t8 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t8.respondent_sequence_number;

CREATE INDEX idx_final_master_respondent_sequence_number    
    ON final_master_table (respondent_sequence_number);


-- Clean up final_master_table
DELETE FROM final_master_table
WHERE respondent_sequence_number NOT IN (
    SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM weight_design
);


-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants who meet any of the diabetes criteria
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
   OR "glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5
   OR "fasting_glucose_mg_dl" >= 126;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants whose age at screening are equal or less than 20 years old
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" > 20;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Outcome: na_normal
-- STEP 4.1.1: Calculate estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1.0), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1.0), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1.0), -0.329) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1.0), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1.0), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1.0), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
        WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
            141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1.0), -0.411) * POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1.0), -1.209) * POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "estimated_glomerular_filtration_rate_egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.1.2: Calculate urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr_raw AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_uacr_mg_g"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- STEP 4.1.3: Determine na_normal based on eGFR and UACR
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_na_normal AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN e."estimated_glomerular_filtration_rate_egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" >= 60
         AND u."urine_albumin_to_creatinine_ratio_uacr_mg_g" < 30 THEN '1'
        ELSE '0'
    END AS "na_normal"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_egfr_raw e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_uacr_raw u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  na."na_normal"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_na_normal na ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = na."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

-- STEP: Create merged final table with design variables + in_analysis flag
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_final_with_weights;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_with_weights AS
SELECT 
    wd."respondent_sequence_number",
    wd."masked_variance_pseudo_psu",
    wd."masked_variance_pseudo_stratum",
    wd."survey_weight",
    CASE WHEN ft."respondent_sequence_number" IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS "in_analysis",
    ft."na_normal"
FROM weight_design wd
LEFT JOIN temp_final_table ft
  ON wd."respondent_sequence_number" = ft."respondent_sequence_number";
  
-- Preview the result
SELECT * FROM temp_final_with_weights;

suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(broom))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tibble))

    identifier_vars <- c("respondent_sequence_number")
    outcome_vars <- c("na_normal")
    weight_vars <- c("survey_weight","masked_variance_pseudo_psu","masked_variance_pseudo_stratum","in_analysis")

    cohort <- df[df$in_analysis == 1, names(df) != "in_analysis"]


    outcome_roles <- c()
    outcome_roles["na_normal"] <- "outcome"


    summarize_variable <- function(column, name) {
    n_unique <- length(unique(na.omit(column)))
    na_count <- sum(is.na(column))

    if (name %in% identifier_vars) {
        category <- "identifier"
        summary_info <- paste0("Total rows = ", nrow(cohort))

    } else if (name %in% outcome_vars) {
        # Assign its special role (e.g., "outcome", "mediator")
        category <- ifelse(!is.na(outcome_roles[name]), outcome_roles[name], "outcome")

        # Generate a summary based on its underlying data type
        underlying_type <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (underlying_type == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (underlying_type %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }

    

    } else if (name %in% weight_vars) {
        category <- "weight"
        summary_info <- "Weight variable" 
    

    } else {
        # This block handles regular predictors
        category <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (category == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (category %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }
    }

    data.frame(
        variable   = name,
        category   = category,
        n_unique   = n_unique,
        na_count   = na_count,
        summary    = summary_info,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    }

    report <- bind_rows(
    lapply(names(cohort), function(col_name) {
        summarize_variable(cohort[[col_name]], col_name)
    })
    )

    identifier_vars  <- report$variable[report$category == "identifier"]
    cat("Identifier Variables:\n");   print(identifier_vars);   cat("\n")

    weight_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "weight"]; cat("Weight Variables: "); print(weight_vars); psu_var <- weight_vars[grepl("psu", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("PSU:", psu_var); stratum_var <- weight_vars[grepl("stratum", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("Stratum:", stratum_var); weight_var <- weight_vars[grepl("weight", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("Weight:", weight_var); in_analysis_var <- weight_vars[grepl("in_analysis", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("In Analysis:", in_analysis_var)

    binary_vars      <- report$variable[report$category == "binary"]
    cat("Binary Variables:\n");       print(binary_vars);       cat("\n")
    print(binary_vars)

    categorical_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "categorical"]
    cat("Categorical Variables:\n");  print(categorical_vars);  cat("\n")
    print(categorical_vars)

    numerical_vars   <- report$variable[report$category == "numerical"]
    cat("Numerical Variables:\n");    print(numerical_vars);    cat("\n")
    print(numerical_vars)

    
    

    outcome_var     <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("outcome")]
    cat("Outcome Variables:\n");      print(outcome_var);      cat("\n")
    

    
    library(survey)
    options(survey.lonely.psu = "adjust")
        
    
    group_vars_list <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary")]
    
    # Loop through the variables and ensure they are clean factors in the 'df' dataframe.
    for (var in group_vars_list) {
    if (var %in% names(df)) {
        df[[var]] <- factor(trimws(as.character(df[[var]])))
    }
    }
        
    
    df[[outcome_var]] <- ifelse(tolower(df[[outcome_var]]) == "yes", 1,
                    ifelse(tolower(df[[outcome_var]]) == "no", 0,
                    ifelse(grepl("1", as.character(df[[outcome_var]])), 1,
                    ifelse(grepl("0", as.character(df[[outcome_var]])), 0,
                            as.numeric(df[[outcome_var]])))))


    varformula <- as.formula(paste0("~", outcome_var))
        
    
    survey_design <- svydesign(
        ids     = as.formula(paste0("~", psu_var)),
        strata  = as.formula(paste0("~", stratum_var)),
        weights = as.formula(paste0("~", weight_var)),
        data    = df,
        nest    = TRUE
        )
    survey_design <- subset(survey_design, in_analysis ==1)
        
    
    # Calculate Overall Prevalence.
    total_prev <- svymean(varformula, survey_design, na.rm = TRUE)
    total_conf <- confint(total_prev)

    # Initialize a list to store pairwise results
    pairwise_results <- list()

    # Loop through each grouping variable
    all_strat_results <- lapply(group_vars_list, function(current_group_var) {

        byformula <- as.formula(paste0("~", current_group_var))
        test_formula <- as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ ", current_group_var))

        # Prevalence and confidence intervals
        strat_prev <- svyby(varformula, byformula, survey_design, svymean, na.rm = TRUE)
        strat_conf <- confint(strat_prev)

        strat_result <- as_tibble(strat_prev) %>%
            cbind(strat_conf) %>%
            rename(
                Group = 1,
                prevalence = 2,
                conf_low = `2.5 %`,
                conf_high = `97.5 %`
            ) %>%
            mutate(Variable = current_group_var, .before = 1)

        # --- Global P-value ---
        group_levels <- levels(factor(df[[current_group_var]]))
        num_levels <- length(group_levels)
        global_p_value <- NA_real_

        test_result <- NULL
        test_p <- NA_real_

        if (num_levels == 2) {
            test_result <- tryCatch(
                svyttest(test_formula, survey_design),
                error = function(e) NULL
            )
        } else if (num_levels > 2) {
            chisq_formula <- as.formula(paste0("~", outcome_var, " + ", current_group_var))
            test_result <- tryCatch(
                svychisq(chisq_formula, survey_design),
                error = function(e) NULL
            )
        }

        if (!is.null(test_result)) {
            test_p <- tryCatch(test_result$p.value, error = function(e) NA_real_)
        }
        global_p_value <- test_p

        # Add global p-value only to the first row
        strat_result$`P-value` <- ""
        strat_result$`P-value`[1] <- if (!is.na(global_p_value)) round(global_p_value, 4) else NA_character_

        # Keep only the columns needed in the final result table
        strat_result <- strat_result %>%
            dplyr::select(Variable, Group, prevalence, conf_low, conf_high, `P-value`)

        # --- Pairwise p-values table (kept as before) ---
        pairwise_df <- tibble(
            Variable = character(),
            Comparison = character(),
            `P-value` = numeric()
        )

        if (num_levels > 2) {
            all_pairs <- combn(group_levels, 2, simplify = FALSE)
            for (pair in all_pairs) {
                pair_name <- paste(pair[1], "vs", pair[2], sep = "_")
                pair_design <- subset(survey_design, get(current_group_var) %in% pair)
                test_result <- try(svyttest(test_formula, pair_design), silent = TRUE)
                if (!inherits(test_result, "try-error")) {
                    pairwise_df <- bind_rows(pairwise_df, tibble(
                        Variable = current_group_var,
                        Comparison = pair_name,
                        `P-value` = round(test_result$p.value, 4)
                    ))
                }
            }
        }

        # Store in global list
        pairwise_results[[current_group_var]] <<- pairwise_df

        return(strat_result)
    })

    
    # Combine into a final result table
    total_result_df <- tibble(
        Variable = "Overall",
        Group = "Overall",
        prevalence = as.numeric(coef(total_prev)),
        conf_low = as.numeric(total_conf[1]),
        conf_high = as.numeric(total_conf[2]),
        `P-value` = ""
    )

    combined_strat_df <- bind_rows(all_strat_results)
    result_table <- bind_rows(total_result_df, combined_strat_df) %>%
        dplyr::select(Variable, Group, prevalence, conf_low, conf_high, `P-value`)

    # Combine pairwise p-values into one data frame (unchanged)
    pairwise_pvalue_table <- bind_rows(pairwise_results)
